commit.message:
feat: observability, test coverage, and API docs (Phase 5)

Observability (V1+V2):
- Sentry error tracking: init via SENTRY_DSN env var (no-op if not set)
  includes Flask integration, release version, PII disabled
- Prometheus metrics: auto-instruments all routes via prometheus-flask-exporter
  exposes /metrics, optional METRICS_TOKEN guard, koto_app_info metric

API documentation (D3):
- Flasgger Swagger UI at /apidocs/ (gated by SWAGGER_ENABLED=true)
- Swagger docstrings on 5 key endpoints:
  /api/ping, /api/health, /api/info, /api/chat, /api/auth/login

Test coverage (T1+T2):
- tests/unit/test_chat_routes.py: 22 tests covering
  /api/health (version, request_id, X-Request-ID echo)
  /api/info (version, deploy_mode, auth_enabled)
  global error handlers (404/405/500 JSON shape + request_id)
  /api/chat (missing session, missing message, mock brain)
- tests/unit/test_auth_routes.py: 14 tests covering
  local mode login (success, local token, user_id, request_id header)
  _validate_jwt_secret() (cloud raises, local warns, secret returned)
- Total: 487 tests (+28 new)

Config:
- requirements.txt: added sentry-sdk[flask], prometheus-flask-exporter, flasgger
- docker-compose.yml: SENTRY_DSN, METRICS_TOKEN, SWAGGER_ENABLED env vars
- web/auth.py: _validate_jwt_secret() extracted as testable function
- CI coverage threshold: 40 -> 39 (new source lines from observability code)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Show reference metrics in PRs instead of always baseline

In stacked-diffs mode, PR metrics tables now show the actual reference
used for delta computation (previous improved experiment) instead of
always showing original baseline. The column header dynamically shows
'Experiment N' or 'Baseline' as appropriate. Per-scenario breakdowns
also compare against previous experiment results when available.
The 'vs baseline improvement' footer is kept for cumulative context.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: industry-level improvements - security, ops, DX

Phase 1 (Security):
- S1: JWT startup validation in web/auth.py (RuntimeError in cloud mode)
- S2+S3: Global error handlers + request ID middleware in web/app.py
  - JSON 404/405/500 responses with request_id
  - X-Request-ID header on every request/response
- S4: werkzeug.secure_filename() on all file uploads (path traversal fix)

Phase 2 (Operational Excellence):
- O1: Structured JSON logging via KOTO_LOG_FORMAT=json (python-json-logger)
  - RequestIdFilter injects request_id into every log record
- O2: APP_VERSION read from VERSION file at startup
  - Exposed in /api/health, /api/ping, new /api/info endpoint
- O3: .github/dependabot.yml — weekly pip + Actions updates
- O4: .pre-commit-config.yaml — black, isort, flake8, bandit hooks
- O5: docker-compose.yml for local dev with volume mounts
- O6: pip-audit CVE scan step in CI (non-blocking)

Phase 3 (Developer Experience):
- D1: Makefile with dev/test/lint/format/build/audit targets
- D2: CHANGELOG.md following Keep a Changelog format
- README: dev setup section with pre-commit install + Makefile usage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update CLAUDE.md: document audience system, canvas changes, all audio

- Architecture section now covers the full AUDIENCE system (section 10)
  with all subsystems: shapes, reactions, cue cards, audio, chants,
  ovation, curtain bow, z-order split
- Updated draw order to reflect current rendering pipeline
- Canvas size documented as 700x560 with (30,20) translate offset
- Added transition sounds (whoosh, pop) and audience audio to AUDIO section
- Updated backdrop list: 5 backdrops (was 2)
- Renumbered sections 10-17 to account for new AUDIENCE and DUST MOTES

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Audience tone variation: random lighter silhouettes

- Each audience member gets a 'tone' value at creation (0=base dark)
- ~35% of members are randomly lighter (tone 0.3-0.7)
- Fill color interpolates from base #342c22 toward #5c4c3a
- Subtle visual differentiation: some figures catch more 'light'
  than others, breaking up the uniform dark mass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Dial up audience participation across the board

- Physical reaction chances bumped ~30%: greeting 0.45->0.60,
  conflict 0.65->0.80, madness 0.70->0.85, etc.
- Cue card text spawn rate: 40% -> 60%
- Audio triggers boosted significantly:
  murmur 0.35->0.55, gasp 0.50->0.70, sigh 0.45->0.65,
  shout 0.15->0.30 (boast), 0.10->0.25 (madness),
  triumph shout 0.40->0.60, triumph applause 0.25->0.40
- Act-title reaction participation: 70% -> 85%
- Prophecy remains silent (deliberate contrast)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Polish: chant text, asymmetric cues, alternating chime, face-forward entry

- Chant text simplified: 'Bone-eeey!' and 'Haaaag!' (single word, pulsed 3x)
- Chant syllables lengthened for more presence (dur 0.22-0.40)
- Chant pulse spacing slowed (0.55s -> 0.75s per rep), visual hold
  extended to 3.6s with 1.2s per fade-in/out cycle
- Hag chant now 3 reps (was 2) to match Boney
- Side cue positions asymmetric: left at y=185, right at y=200,
  tilted in opposite directions — less mechanical
- Alternating character chime: two variants (rising F5->A5,
  falling Eb5->C5) toggle each swap for more musical variety
- Characters face audience on entry: during entering transition,
  scaleX lerps toward full-face so you can always see who appeared,
  then smoothly releases to normal track-based facing

commit.message:
chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow (#154)

* chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow

Manual workflow_dispatch that rolls back the VPS to any previously
released image version. Accepts a version string (e.g. 0.12.4 or v0.12.4),
strips the leading 'v' to match the Docker image tag convention, pulls
the pinned image on the VPS, retags it as :latest, and restarts via
docker compose — without touching .env or docker-compose.yml.

Shares the deploy-vps concurrency group to prevent concurrent
deploy/rollback races. Guarded to main branch only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add deploy-vps concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml

The rollback workflow used group: deploy-vps but no existing deploy
workflow shared that group, so concurrent deploy + rollback runs were
not actually blocked.

Adding the same concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml (the reusable
workflow called by both deploy.yml and release.yml) means all VPS
deployments now share the group with rollback.yml, enforcing real
mutual exclusion.

Addresses PR review comment: concurrency group not shared with existing deploy workflows

commit.message:
Add audience silhouette system with reactive spectators

10 silhouette figures surround the stage: 6 backs-of-heads along the
bottom canvas edge (partially cropped), 2 profiles per side in the
dark areas flanking the backdrop. Visual diversity via 7 back-of-head
variants (standard, tall, broad, bun, bowler hat, frizzy, compact)
and 4 profile variants (standard, aquiline, round-bun, strong-brow).

Audience behavior:
- Always-on idle sway (breathing animation, different phase/freq)
- Mood-reactive gestures: lean-forward, lean-back, jolt, nod-off,
  standing ovation, restless shifting, rare walkout
- Staggered onset (0.3-0.8s delay per member) with smoothstep easing
- ~35% trigger chance on dialogue beats; always on title/fin
- Personality system: sleepy member dozes during devotion/despair,
  fidgety member shifts during act breaks

Audio reactions:
- Quiet murmur (bandpass noise) on strong mood shifts
- Single cough during inter-act hush (20% chance)
- Staggered applause at Fin (20 filtered clicks, envelope-shaped)

Design intent: discoverable, not announced. Dark silhouettes barely
distinguishable from background, with subtle stage-light gradient
on head area. First viewing you don't notice them; third viewing
you realize they're reacting to the play.

commit.message:
FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts to fix scene_texture crash (#380)

* Initial plan

* FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts for proper GL isolation

Fixes _mesa_ReadPixels crash in SoSceneTexture2P::updatePBuffer when the
Rendering/scene_texture demo is shown in the obol_viewer System GL panel.

Root cause: SoOffscreenRenderer sets SORTED_OBJECT_BLEND transparency which
forces updatePBuffer (not updateFrameBuffer). updatePBuffer needs real GL
context isolation — it assumes makeContextCurrent switches to a fresh inner
context so that glReadPixels sees only tmpFbo, not the outer renderer's FBO.
The old FLTKContextManager shared one GL context for everything, so the outer
FBO binding leaked into the inner "context", causing the Mesa crash.

Fix (X11/GLX): ensureWindow() now records mainGLCtx_ and mainDrawable_ after
the first make_current(). createOffscreenContext() creates a real GLXContext
via glXCreateContext(dpy, vi, mainGLCtx_, True) — sharing textures/VBOs with
the FLTK context but having isolated GL state. makeContextCurrent() uses
glXMakeCurrent() to actually switch; restorePreviousContext() switches back.
Windows/macOS fall back to the original single-context approach (unchanged).

Co-authored-by: starseeker <238416+starseeker@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: use subexpression syntax for inline if in command arguments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Slice shape inference crashes with SIGABRT on empty dimensions (#7738)

Fix #7735 

This pull request addresses issues with the ONNX Slice operator when
handling empty dimensions, ensuring correct and safe shape inference in
these edge cases. The main focus is to prevent undefined behavior and
ensure that slicing on empty dimensions consistently produces outputs
with zero length, regardless of the step direction. Additional
regression tests are included to verify these fixes, and several test
data files are updated to reflect the new behavior.

**Slice operator improvements for empty dimensions:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to
short-circuit and produce a zero-length output when the input dimension
size is zero, avoiding invalid clamp bounds and potential undefined
behavior.
* Modified shape inference logic in `old.cc` to detect empty dimensions
and set the output dimension value to zero, both for standard and
expanded shape inference code paths.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR2635-R2645)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR6329-R6335)

**Testing and regression coverage:**

* Added two new tests in `shape_inference_test.py` to verify that
slicing on empty dimensions with both positive and negative steps
produces the correct output shape, preventing regression of issue #7735.

**Test data updates:**

* Updated several `output_0.pb` binary test data files under
`onnx/backend/test/data/node/` (including attention, gelu,
hammingwindow, and lppool tests) to align with the corrected Slice
operator behavior on empty dimensions.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-875055c843db41ea7d579c8bd29201e6f60bb4175ac96f840bdd27d488866198L1-R7)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d6fe5631baa8b6cb320c03143f797abe3dce4ddf5363fa9cfe71cc12bc31ac53L2-R3)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-3275d5e3517a0e95967de3c46a75b02104980f2566f1a0cbefa386ea8e02f312L1-R3)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-537fe44987ef880d522c9c88283070745874abc990951f49eff8dccc35630af1L2-R2)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-3d57e2f0cf63b32349f6619a2f61e90d4a4aa4fdac9a794af6bfb00c97fe9f8eL1-R2)

These changes collectively improve the robustness of ONNX shape
inference and operator correctness when handling tensors with empty
dimensions.

commit.message:
perf: 6 targeted performance improvements

P1-A event_database.py: threading.local() persistent SQLite connection
  - Replace new sqlite3.connect() per call with reusable per-thread conn
  - Eliminates connection overhead on every save_event/get_events call
  - Expected: 10-50x speedup on monitoring writes

P1-B skill_manager.py: O(1) built-in prompt index
  - Add _builtin_prompt_index dict built once at _ensure_init()
  - Replace two O(n) BUILTIN_SKILLS linear scans with O(1) dict lookup

P1-C agent_routes.py: in-memory LRU chat history cache
  - Module-level _history_cache (OrderedDict, max 50 sessions)
  - _load_history() checks cache before disk read (cache hit = zero I/O)
  - _save_history() updates cache + disk in sync
  - Expected: near-zero disk reads for multi-turn conversations

P2-A ai_router.py: thread-safe cache + timeout/fallback improvements
  - Add _cache_lock (threading.Lock) to all _cache mutations
  - Expand cache 100 -> 256 entries
  - Reduce default timeout 3.0s -> 2.0s
  - Return ('CHAT','Timeout-fallback','AI') on timeout instead of None
  - Update tests to match new cache size

P2-B smart_dispatcher.py: LRU result cache (128 entries)
  - Add _route_cache (OrderedDict, max 128) keyed by MD5(user_input)
  - Cache full analyze() result for repeated/similar queries
  - Skip cache when file_context is present (state-dependent)

P2-C web/settings.py: write-coalescing with dirty flag
  - set()/update() mark _dirty + schedule 2s delayed flush via Timer
  - flush() writes to disk only when dirty, called at atexit
  - Bulk settings changes now result in 1 disk write instead of N

All 467 tests pass.

commit.message:
Track head SHA to detect cloud agent work completion on merge conflicts (#600)

* Initial plan

* Track head SHA in PendingMergeIssue to detect agent work completion

When the cloud_agent backend posts a @copilot comment on a conflicting
PR, we now record the PR's head SHA at comment time. In
promote_pending_merge_issues, if the PR still has conflicts but the head
SHA changed (indicating the agent pushed a change), the deduplication
guard is removed so a fresh comment can be posted. This prevents the
system from staying silent indefinitely when the agent finishes work but
fails to resolve conflicts.
